items.title: USDA final SNAP retailer stocking rule: updated staple-food standards take effect Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service says the updated SNAP retailer stocking standards published May 8, 2026 must be followed starting Nov. 4, 2026. The eligibility page highlights numeric requirements including at least 36 staple food items, with three varieties in each of four staple-food categories and three stocking units per variety, plus at least one perishable variety in two categories.

items.title: Education Dept. posts effective dates for Workforce Pell and new Pell ineligibility rules: July 1, 2026
items.summary: Federal Student Aid says Workforce Pell provisions take effect July 1, 2026. The same July 1, 2026 effective date also applies to Federal Pell Grant ineligibility provisions described in the announcement.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (U.S. Department of Education)

items.title: Massachusetts says SUN Bucks (Summer EBT) 2026 benefits began issuing June 1: $120 per eligible child
items.summary: Massachusetts’ SUN Bucks FAQ says the first issuance of SUN Bucks 2026 benefits was June 1, 2026. The same page states the benefit is $120 per eligible child as a one-time, lump-sum payment for summer months.

items.title: KFF Health News: new federal Medicaid work rules could affect 18.5 million adults; CBO estimates 5.3 million uninsured by 2034
items.summary: KFF Health News reports that the One Big Beautiful Bill Act could require about 18.5 million Medicaid adults across 42 states and D.C. to document work or qualifying activities to keep coverage, unless exempt. The story also cites a Congressional Budget Office estimate that 5.3 million enrollees would become uninsured by 2034 due to Medicaid work requirements.

items.title: FAFSA reminder: 2026–27 aid year federal filing deadline is June 30, 2027 (but many schools/states have earlier priority dates)
items.summary: A FAFSA explainer notes the 2026–27 FAFSA covers the award period from July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027 and lists the federal deadline to submit as June 30, 2027. It also warns that colleges and states can have earlier priority deadlines that may affect access to limited grant aid.

items.title: USDA FNS posts new SNAP resources in late May/early June, including a June 1 Federal Register information-collection notice
items.summary: USDA’s FNS resources listing shows a June 1, 2026 entry for an information collection titled “FNS-245 SNAP Negative Case Action Review Schedule.” The same list includes late-May SNAP items dated May 27, 2026 covering information collections related to SNAP incentive waiver requests and to state options for standard utility allowances and self-employment income.

items.title: IRS publishes Internal Revenue Bulletin 2026-24; no new EITC/CTC guidance in this issue
items.summary: The IRS’s Internal Revenue Bulletin 2026-24 includes items like Rev. Rul. 2026-11 (federal rates and a Low-Income Housing Credit table) and Notice 2026-33 (qualified long-term care distributions). A review of the issue did not find new guidance specifically addressing the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Child Tax Credit (CTC).
